title: 永久底层：硅谷的AI从业者普遍认为，普通人已经“完蛋了” | 原文
content: The article discusses a recent Silicon Valley visit by a Chinese investor who reviewed the AI industry’s extreme competition. It describes how firms like Meta are using competitors’ tools such as Anthropic’s Claude, abandoning code‑security norms, and how engineers spend nearly their entire salary on token usage. One engineer remarks that even Silicon Valley cannot keep up with itself, highlighting the relentless, unsustainable arms race dominating the AI sector.

normalizedTitle: 永久底层 硅谷的ai从业者普遍认为 普通人已经“完蛋了” 原文

title: Welcome to Maintainer Month: Celebrating the people behind the code
content: Maintainer Month celebrates open‑source maintainers by offering new tools, resources, and community events. Projects now get granular contribution limits, PR archiving, and access to guides on accessibility and workflow control. Partner organizations such as Sentry, OpenJS Foundation, and Daytona provide tools, compute credits, and conference tickets. Over 20 events—conferences, hackathons, and discussions—focus on security, licensing, and corporate support. A vetted Maintainer Community and partner packs extend support, while financial sponsorship and public recognition help sustain maintainers’ vital work.
source: The GitHub Blog - Abigail Cabunoc Mayes
eventKey: welcome-maintainer-month-celebrating-people-behind
normalizedTitle: welcome to maintainer month celebrating the people behind the code
representativeSources: The GitHub Blog - Abigail Cabunoc Mayes

title: 我用飞书多维表做了一个 AI 活动推荐智能体：每天自动催我别错过截止日期！
content: I built an AI activity‑reminder bot in Feishu’s multidimensional table and workflow system. The table stores AI competitions, hackathons, and training camp links, with fields for title, URL, status, start and end dates. A workflow triggers daily at 6 pm, finds upcoming events, formats the list, and sends reminder messages. A second reminder at 9 am distributes AI news. The bot helps participants avoid missing deadlines and can be updated continuously.

title: LearningCircuit/local-deep-research
content: LearningCircuit’s local-deep-research repository develops a Python framework that delivers high accuracy (≈95 %) on SimpleQA using local and cloud LLMs such as Qwen3.6-27B on a 3090. It supports multiple LLM interfaces (llama.cpp, Ollama, Google, etc.) and integrates more than ten search engines, including arXiv, PubMed, and private document sources. Everything runs locally and is encrypted, with 4,876 stars and 460 forks on GitHub.

title: 境外 DNS 服务商 ECS (EDNS Client Subnet) 功能效果完整对比报告(2026 年 05 月） ，供大家参考使用
content: This report compares the ECS (EDNS Client Subnet) routing effectiveness of six DNS providers—AdGuard (unfiltered), Google, AdGuard (filtered), Quad9, OpenDNS, and NextDNS—using DoH queries from Japanese, Chinese, US, and Brazilian /24 subnets for Amazon, GitHub, Netflix, YouTube, Twitch, and Apple. AdGuard unfiltered scores highest (25/30), accurately routing all domains. Google and filtered AdGuard follow closely; Quad9, OpenDNS, and NextDNS perform poorly, especially for GitHub and Netflix. The report also recommends Singapore-based proxies for lowest latency and best ECS determinism.
eventKey: dns-服务商-ecs-edns-client-subnet
normalizedTitle: 境外 dns 服务商 ecs edns client subnet 功能效果完整对比报告 2026 年 05 月 供大家参考使用

title: 大型挂机现场：马斯克的55万英伟达GPU，利用率才11%
content: Elon Musk’s xAI operates about 550,000 NVIDIA GPUs but only uses roughly 11% of them. The low utilization stems from coordination challenges, memory bandwidth limits, network latency, and intermittent training workloads. Larger clusters suffer idle time that worsens efficiency. While other firms like Meta and Google reach 43–46% usage, xAI is working to raise its rate to 50% through infrastructure and software stack improvements. The case highlights that buying GPUs is just the first step; effective usage is the true challenge in AI.

title: 消失的五一假期之——找不到好用的自托管图床，于是自己写了一个（开源 / Docker 一键部署）
content: Developed a self‑hosted image‑hosting service after finding existing options lacking. Built with Go, React, and PostgreSQL, it runs in a single Docker image and can be deployed via docker‑compose. It offers a full management interface, multiple link formats, image‑editing features (format conversion, watermark), OpenAPI docs, Prometheus metrics, and native MCP support. Supports six storage backends (local, S3, OSS, COS, Kodo, WebDAV) and easy integration with tools like ShareX, PicGo, and others. Source available on GitHub and Docker Hub.

title: Last Week in AI #340 - OpenAI vs Musk + Microsoft, DeepSeek v4, Vision Banana
content: The first week of the Musk‑vs‑Altman trial focused on Musk’s testimony, alleging OpenAI’s migration to a for‑profit model and claiming theft of the nonprofit mission. Musk admitted xAI partly used OpenAI’s models, while Brockman testified about an IPO and ownership stakes. Microsoft and OpenAI resolved a partnership dispute, shifting from exclusive to non‑exclusive licensing. DeepSeek released V4 models with large context windows, and DeepMind unveiled Vision Banana, a unified image‑generation and vision‑understanding model.
source: Last Week in AI - Last Week in AI

content: The article critiques how Chinese tourist sites use AI‑driven photo and video services—smart cameras, drones, and theft‑like facial‑recognition—to upsell experiences and monetise visitors’ images. It details the low‑quality “crime‑scene”‑style AI outputs, extra charges for watermark removal, and the risks of data misuse and privacy violations. Legal challenges against default image‑rights clauses and new regulatory guidelines illustrate consumer pushback, raising concerns about the ethics of AI‑powered “smart” tourism.

title: Anthropic 接近与黑石、高盛等达成 15 亿美元的合资协议
content: Anthropic is negotiating a $1.5 billion joint venture with Black Rock, Goldman Sachs, and other Wall Street firms. Each partner, including Anthropic and Hellman Friedman, is expected to contribute roughly $300 million. The venture aims to fund private‑equity‑backed companies and expand Anthropic’s AI capabilities. The partnership reflects the growing interest of financial institutions in AI-driven investment opportunities.
source: Readhub - 每日早报 - 财联社

title: 英伟达黄仁勋痛批 Anthropic CEO，呼吁 AI 行业领袖慎言慎行、以事实为据
content: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang criticized Anthropic CEO Daniel Amodei in a podcast, arguing that AI leaders should speak cautiously and base claims on facts. Huang disputed Amodei’s assertion that AI could replace 50 % of entry-level white‑collar jobs in the next few years and dismissed dramatic warnings that AI could destroy humanity, calling them irresponsible. He urged the industry to avoid “god‑like” rhetoric and to focus on realistic, evidence‑based discussions about AI’s economic and societal impact.
source: Readhub - 每日早报 - 钛媒体

title: 马斯克寻求就 OpenAI 诉讼达成和解
content: Elon Musk contacted OpenAI co‑founder Greg Brockman two days before a court hearing to discuss a settlement in his lawsuit against OpenAI and Microsoft. He is seeking leadership changes and a $150 billion damages award, arguing that OpenAI stole his ideas. The lawsuit and its demands have attracted media coverage and criticism from OpenAI, which accuses Musk of obstructing AGI development.
source: Readhub - 每日早报 - 和讯网
